rule_metadata.author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
rule_metadata.title: Suspicious PowerShell Cmdlets - WMI

rules.rule_text:
title: Suspicious PowerShell Cmdlets - WMI
id: e29d3301-3b64-4305-9e91-8aabfbef2015
status: experimental
description: "There are numerous default PowerShell cmdlets that allow administrators\
  \ to leverage WMI via \nPowerShell. Both adversaries and administrators use these\
  \ cmdlets to query the operating \nsystem or execute commands, either locally or\
  \ remotely. Cmdlets like Get-WMIObject are often \nused for reconnaissance. Part\
  \ of the RedCanary 2024 Threat Detection Report.\n"
references:
- https://redcanary.com/threat-detection-report/techniques/windows-management-instrumentation/
author: RedCanary, Sigma formatting by Micah Babinski
date: 2024/03/21
tags:
- attack.execution
- attack.t1047
- attack.t1059
- attack.t1059.001
logsource:
  category: process_creation
  product: windows
detection:
  selection:
    Image|endswith: \powershell.exe
    CommandLine|contains:
    - invoke-wmimethod
    - invoke-cimmethod
    - get-wmiobject
    - get-ciminstance
    - wmiclass
  condition: selection
falsepositives:
- Unknown
level: low
